title: DoTA-seq V3.1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eimq7","text":"This protocol describes the process of DoTA-seq generating a single cell sequencing library from a cell suspension. This workflow can be performed in two days, with the PCR step happening overnight. Before beginning this workflow make sure to have:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7gmdm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kj5r","text":"1. The necessary microfluidics devices prepared and ready to go","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1123m","text":"2. The multiplex DoTA-seq target primers validated to work together without generating large molecular weight primer dimers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9opsn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bgea4","text":"Please read the publication for further details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3qrjk","text":"Strongly recommend all pre-PCR steps (setting up reagents, washing gels) to be done in a PCR Clean hood. This has two purposes: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9s28u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"35vf1","text":"1. Reduce PCR contamination of templates which can strongly effect single-cell PCR reactions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dl331","text":"2. Reduce dust contamination of reagents which can clog devices and cause failures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare a cell suspension by washing twice in 1mL of PBS 0.1% Tween20 Contributed by users by spinning down at 5000 x g, °C, 1 minute
Resuspend cells in 100 µL PBS 0.1% Tween 20 Contributed by users
Add 1 µL SYBR Green Thermo Fisher Scientific 10,000X dye to the cells to stain them
Count cells using a hemacytometer using the SYBR signal, calculate concentration of the cell suspension.
(Optional) Stain with Cellbrite Fix 555 Biotium Catalog ##30088 to get a cell membrane/wall stain

Make 200 µL Hydrogel Precursor Solution - Mix together in a tube: 100 µL Acrylamide P212121 monomer in water 25 Mass / % volume 15 µL NN′-Bis(acryloyl)cystamine Santa Cruz Biotechnology Catalog #sc-215506 (CAS: 60984-57-8) in Methanol 5 Mass / % volume 10 µL Ammonium persulfate Contributed by users Catalog #A3678 10 Mass / % volume 75 µL Cell suspension diluted in PBS ( a total of 7e6 cells to achieve a final concentration of 3.5e7 cells/mL in the total solution) Vortex Vigorously to Mix

Prepare and Load the Syringes with the gel sample and 600 µL Biorad Evagreen Droplet Oil Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog ##1864005 and connect to the microfluidic devices by following this protocol Loading Syringes to Inject into Microfluidics Device by freeman.lan
Connect the syringes to the inlets of the DoTA-seq Step 1 Microfluidics Device and Run the syringe pumps at 500uL/hr for the gel syringe, and 900uL/hr for the oil syringe.

Collect gel droplets for 00:20:00 in a 1mL tube. Sometimes the initial droplet formation produces polydisperse droplets. In this case, wait 2 min for the bad emulsion to leave the outlet tubing into a waste tube, then begin collecting in the collection tube.

Make 200 µL Gel Polymerization Oil - mix together in a tube: 195 µL Biorad Evagreen Droplet Oil Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog ##1864005 5 µL TEMED (Tetramethyl-ethulenediamine) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T9281

Add the Gel polymerization oil to the collected droplets, invert slowly 3 times to mix, and Incubate the tube containing droplets at 37 °C for 00:10:00 to complete polymerization of the gel matrix. You can now look at the emulsion under the microscope using Countess slides Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #C10228 to determine the encapsulation ratio of your cells. SYBRGreen and CF555 signal should be concordant and correspond to cells.
Pulse spin the emulsion in a centrifuge to close pack the emulsion and drain the oil to the bottom of the tube.
Use a pipette to remove the oil from the bottom of the tube, leaving just the emulsion
Add 200 µL Perfluorooctanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #370533 to break the emulsion Vortex, then Wait 00:01:00 for the emulsion to break.
Pulse spin again and remove the oil in the bottom of the tube with a pipette.
Add 1000 µL of Acetone Contributed by users to the tube. Mix by inverting 5 times, Wait 00:00:10 then remove with a pipette. The gels should begin to flocculate and dehydrate.

Add 1000 µL of Isopropanol Contributed by users Mix by inverting 5 times, Wait 00:00:10 then remove with a pipette. The gels should dehydrate and become hard. Note: Do not wait too long as it could cause the gels to irreversibly aggregate into clumps.

Resuspend in 1000 µL PBS EDTA 1mM Tween20 2% w/v Contributed by users The gels can be stored at 4 °C for several days without changing DoTA-seq results. You can now look at the gels under the microscope using Countess slides Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #C10228 to determine the encapsulation ratio of your cells. For SYBR Staining, you should first wash a small aliquot in PBS 2% Tween to remove background SYBR+ oil droplets before visualization on the microscope. You should see some loss in CF555 signal as the acetone and alcohol wash removes some bacterial membranes.
Wash gels 3 times in 1000 µL 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline ) (No Tween) by centrifugation at 500 x g, °C, 30 seconds each time

Make a Enzymatic Lysis Solution by adding: 20 mg Lysozyme from chicken egg white Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L6876 100 µL 1 mg/mL MetaPolyzyme Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #MAC4L-5MG 900 µL 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline ) (No Tween)
Resuspend the gels in this lysis solution. Incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00
Wash the gels 3 times in 1000 µL PBS 0.1% Tween20 Contributed by users

Make a SDS Lysis solution by adding: 20 µL 20 mg/mL Proteinase K solution, 20 mg ml − 1 Ambion Catalog #AM2546 100 µL 10% SDS Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #161-0146 880 µL TE Buffer (Tris 10mM EDTA 1mM pH 8) Contributed by users
Resuspend the gels in this SDS Lysis solution, incubate at 55 °C for 01:00:00

Wash the gels three times in 1000 µL TE 2% Tween-20 Contributed by users Note: Use 2% Tween 20, not 0.1% Tween These gels can be stored at 4 °C in TE 2% Tween-20 Contributed by users for several days without impacting DoTA-seq results. You can now look at the gels under the microscope using Countess slides Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #C10228 to determine the encapsulation ratio and lysis efficiency of your cells. It is advised to restain with SYBR and CF555 to get best signal. Lysed cells should exhibit SYBR signal but no CF555 Signal.
Wash the gels three times in 1000 µL Pre-injection buffer 10mM HEPES pH 7.5 Pluronic 0.1% Contributed by users
Resuspend gels in 100 µL Pre-injection buffer 10mM HEPES pH 7.5 Pluronic 0.1% Contributed by users

Load the gels into a syringe following the protocol described in this excellent visual protocol. An Ultrahigh-throughput Microfluidic Platform for Single-cell Genome Sequencing.. Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE. Alternatively, for a simpler version, you can also use a P200 pipette to directly pipette the gel into a syringe backfilled with HFE7500.

Generate a PCR Master Mix (This mix gives about ~10,000 cells per library - Scale up as required) 25 µL ddPCR Supermix for probes (no dUTP) Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1863024 0.4 µL 50 micromolar (µM) P7 Primer mixed with Barrev primer at 5 micromolar (µM) 0.4 µL 50 micromolar (µM) P5 Primer with appropriate I5 index 0.2 µL, Variable 10 micromolar (µM) DoTA-seq multiplex primer mix ( 10-20nM final concentration per prime r) 0.2 µL, Variable 10 micromolar (µM) 16S DoTA-seq primers ( 10-20nM final concentration ) 0.5 µL, Variable 1 picomolar (pM), Freshly diluted from 500pM stock Barcode Oligo 0.25 µL 500 millimolar (mM), Single use aliquots DTT Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D0632 The ratio of 16S to DoTA-seq target primers mix can be varied depending on the relative amplification efficiencies. The best way to determine is to start from equal concentrations, then adjust based on the sequencing results (do most cells contain more 16S reads than target reads?) IMPORTANT - BARCODE CONCENTRATIONS MAY NEED TO BE MEASURED Typically, 0.5uL of 1pM barcode will give approximately 1 barcode for every 10 droplets. However, it is best to measure the barcode encapsulation rate by making PCR droplets containing the barcodes at the expected dilution and P7 and Barrev primers targeting the barcode for amplification. Visualize the resulting PCR emulsion using SYBRgreen staining under the microscope to obtain the real encapsulation ratio. Typically, the real barcode concentration can be ~5 fold off from the expected concentration based on manufacturer's labelling. Barcode oligos should always be freshly diluted from 500pM to 1pM before use, as we have found gradual loss of barcodes over time in a 1pM solution.

Load the PCR mastermix into the syringe following this protocol Load 500 µL of Biorad Evagreen Droplet Oil Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog ##1864005 into a syringe following this protocol Loading Syringes to Inject into Microfluidics Device by freeman.lan

Connect the syringes to the DoTA-seq Step 2 microfluidics device. Run the syringe pumps at 200uL/hr for the gel and PCR mastermix, and 800uL/hr for the oil syringe. Collect droplets in an Axygen® 0.2 mL Maxymum Recovery® Thin Wall PCR Tubes Corning Catalog #PCR-02-L-C for 00:07:00 for every 25 µL of PCR mastermix or until the PCR mastermix runs out.
Use a pipette to remove the oil in the PCR tube, leaving just the emulsion layer (it's okay to have a little bit of oil remaining).
Thermocycle the PCR emulsion as follows: 95 °C 5 min 40 cycles of: 95 °C 30s 72 °C 10s 60 °C 5 min 72 °C 30s Final incubation of: 72 °C 10min 12 °C Hold All ramp times are at 1 °C per second
Keep the emulsion on ice to prevent polymerase activity Add 25 µL EDTA (0.5 M), pH 8.0 Life Technologies Catalog #AM9260G to the emulsion Vortex the emulsion to mix

Add 25 µL HFE 7500 Perfluorinated Oil Contributed by users (CAS: 297730-93-9) to the emulsion Add 25 µL Perfluorooctanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #370533 to the emulsion Vortex the emulsion to mix

Wait 00:01:00 , then pulse centrifuge to separate the PCR mix from the oil. Note: If you do not see a clear separation of two clear phases and no emulsion remaining, repeat step 35. Transfer the top aqueous phase to a new 1mL tube.

Add 20 µL 1 Molarity (M) TCEP-HCl Gold Biotechnology Catalog #TCEP to the tube and vortex to completely decrosslink any remaining gels. You should be unable to obtain any "jellyish" substance by centrifugation! If there is any jellyish substance left it is not fully de-crosslinked. ADD MORE TCEP.
Clean up the PCR reaction using the DNA Clean & Concentrator™-5 Zymo Research Catalog #D4003 kit. Elute in 50 µL Elution Buffer.
Remove primer dimers and free barcodes using the SPRIselect reagent kit Beckman Coulter Catalog #B23317 with 0.7X volume of beads.

Check the resulting library for primer dimers using TapeStation (Agilent, G2991AA, https://www.agilent.com/en/product/tapestation-automated-electrophoresis/tapestation-instruments/4200-tapestation-system-228263 ) with a High Sensitivity D1000 ScreenTape Agilent Technologies Catalog #5067-5584 Other high sensitivity capillary electrophoresis methods will also work. There should be minimal primer dimers on the trace. Below is an example of an acceptable trace. Example of an acceptable Tapestation trace.

Quantify the library using a qPCR library quantification kit such as NEBNext Library Quant Kit for Illumina - 100 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E7630S Note that you must use a PCR based library quantification kit as not all amplicons contain all the adaptors for sequencing and therefore will throw off sequence non-specific forms of quantification!
Sequence the library on an Illumina sequencer using Custom Sequencing Primers listed here. DoTA-seq-Oligo-Sequences.xlsx
Add 1000 µL of HFE 7500 Perfluorinated Oil Contributed by users (CAS: 297730-93-9) to the tube. Mix by inverting 5 times, Wait 00:00:10 then remove with a pipette.
creator: freeman.lan
